nodes.label: Statement One: Cursor Track (AI-Native Design System)
nodes.norm_label: statement one: cursor track (ai-native design system)

nodes.label: Statement Two: Vultr Track (Web-Based Enterprise Agent)
nodes.norm_label: statement two: vultr track (web-based enterprise agent)

nodes.label: Statement Three: Crusoe Track (Live Situational Model Agent)
nodes.norm_label: statement three: crusoe track (live situational model agent)

nodes.label: Statement Four: Google DeepMind Track (Load-Bearing Gemini Primitives)
nodes.norm_label: statement four: google deepmind track (load-bearing gemini primitives)

nodes.label: Hackathon Judging Criteria (Impact 25%, Demo 50%, Creativity 15%, Pitch 10%)
nodes.norm_label: hackathon judging criteria (impact 25%, demo 50%, creativity 15%, pitch 10%)

nodes.label: Reclaim (Telecom SLA Credit Recovery Agent)
nodes.rationale: Chosen because auditing SLA credits manually across a 2,000-store telecom portfolio is too slow, so credits are missed; a bounded enterprise workflow beats a chat-with-your-contract demo for the Vultr track.

nodes.label: Non-Negotiable Demo Proof (Plan Changes Backend Path)
nodes.rationale: The judged demo must prove agency by showing one shallow path that skips exclusion retrieval, one deep path that triggers second retrieval, and one operational-safety case; every dollar must come from deterministic tools, not model text.
nodes.norm_label: non-negotiable demo proof (plan changes backend path)

nodes.label: Glass-Box Reasoning Rail
nodes.rationale: Not decorative but the proof surface: every rail card is driven by a real backend event, and skipped steps stay visible as dimmed cards because the agent deciding NOT to act is as important as acting.

nodes.label: Confidence Formula (0.4 retrieval + 0.4 ambiguity + 0.2 billing)
nodes.rationale: Published as a thresholded routing rule (below 0.80 routes to Needs review) so confidence reads as auditable math rather than an LLM vibe.

nodes.label: Deterministic Dollar Math Principle
nodes.rationale: No LLM output may be the source of a dollar amount, deadline calculation, prior-credit determination, or contract precedence decision; deterministic tools make every memo dollar defensible.

nodes.label: Carrier-Ready Credit Memo
nodes.rationale: The output is framed as a claim packet, not an answer or dashboard card, to counter the judge objection that the output is shallow.

nodes.label: Worked Example CKT-ATL-014 (87 min, 99.80% vs 99.9%, $124 credit)
nodes.norm_label: worked example ckt-atl-014 (87 min, 99.80% vs 99.9%, $124 credit)

nodes.label: Vultr Object Storage (Docs, Highlights, Golden Traces)
nodes.norm_label: vultr object storage (docs, highlights, golden traces)

nodes.label: Vultr Serverless Inference Vector Store (Retrieval Layer)
nodes.rationale: Preferred semantic retrieval layer with a local Chroma mirror kept as a judging fallback against provisioning or venue-network risk.
nodes.norm_label: vultr serverless inference vector store (retrieval layer)

nodes.label: Agent Loop (Plan, Retrieve, Compute, Classify, Assemble)
nodes.norm_label: agent loop (plan, retrieve, compute, classify, assemble)

nodes.label: Planner (selected_stages, evidence_needed, rationale, stop_conditions)
nodes.rationale: The planner controls optional stages so the executed backend path visibly changes per case, which is the core counterproof to 'this is just RAG'.

nodes.label: Deterministic Tools Suite (uptime, credit, billing, deadline, precedence, confidence, memo)
nodes.rationale: All tools return fixed JSON outputs; acceptance rule forbids LLM output as the source of any dollar amount, deadline, prior-credit, or precedence decision.
nodes.norm_label: deterministic tools suite (uptime, credit, billing, deadline, precedence, confidence, memo)

nodes.label: API Shape (/health, /run-portfolio, /cases SSE events, memo, approve)
nodes.norm_label: api shape (/health, /run-portfolio, /cases sse events, memo, approve)

nodes.label: Data Model (documents, chunks, circuits, outage_intervals, cases, audit_events, approvals)
nodes.norm_label: data model (documents, chunks, circuits, outage_intervals, cases, audit_events, approvals)

nodes.label: Golden-Run Trace Replay Fallback
nodes.rationale: Trace replay from Object Storage is used only if live API or network conditions fail, keeping the demo peak bulletproof without faking the pipeline.

nodes.label: Single-Screen Three-Pane Layout (Queue, Rail, Memo)
nodes.rationale: The product should feel like a revenue-assurance workbench, dense and receipt-heavy, not an AI chat app; every dollar must visibly connect to a backend event, a cited clause, and a human approval.

nodes.label: Citation Chip and Source Drawer Experience
nodes.rationale: The citation chip is 'the money shot' and must open instantly; pre-rendered highlight assets are used for on-camera circuits because live PDF span mapping is a stretch, not a dependency.
nodes.norm_label: citation chip and source drawer experience

nodes.label: Judge Risk Register (8 Objections with Counterproofs)
nodes.rationale: The highest-leverage scoring improvement is not more features but making planning, branching, telecom identifiers, and Vultr services visible in the first 30 seconds.
nodes.norm_label: judge risk register (8 objections with counterproofs)

nodes.label: Frontend-Backend Contract (health, run, SSE events, memo, approve)
nodes.norm_label: frontend-backend contract (health, run, sse events, memo, approve)

nodes.label: Local Fallback and Golden-Trace Replay Mode
nodes.rationale: The self-contained deterministic trace is used only when the API is unreachable, when ?fallback is present, or when the user clicks Replay, keeping live mode primary.
nodes.norm_label: local fallback and golden-trace replay mode

nodes.label: Approval Form with Typed Audit Reason (#approvalForm)
nodes.norm_label: approval form with typed audit reason (#approvalform)

nodes.label: Designer 1 Visual System (OKLCH Palette, Typography, Motion, A11y)
nodes.author: Designer 1
nodes.rationale: Green is reserved for approved recovery only, amber for risk, blue for citations/current action, so color semantics reinforce the trust story instead of decorating it.
nodes.norm_label: designer 1 visual system (oklch palette, typography, motion, a11y)

nodes.label: Designer 2 Demo Polish Blueprint (Camera Path, Microcopy)
nodes.author: Designer 2
nodes.rationale: Scoped to the demo surface: make the agentic proof, telecom credibility, Vultr fit, deterministic math, citations, and approval impossible to miss in 60 seconds; treat the counter as recoverable exposure until human approval turns it green.
nodes.norm_label: designer 2 demo polish blueprint (camera path, microcopy)

nodes.label: Designer 3 UI Critique (24/40 Health Score, P0-P2 Fixes)
nodes.author: Designer 3
nodes.rationale: Verdict: right story architecture but editorial styling undermines demo legibility; top two fixes are compressing chrome so rail cards are immediately visible and remapping color semantics (green=approved, amber=risk, blue=citation).
nodes.norm_label: designer 3 ui critique (24/40 health score, p0-p2 fixes)

nodes.label: Authorship-Time Inversion
nodes.rationale: Intervene inside the tool causing drift (Cursor's agent) before drift reaches a diff, instead of auditing after the fact on CI or post-merge — the sharpest reading of the brief for a Cursor-sponsored track.

nodes.label: Promote-to-System Loop
nodes.rationale: A rule engine enforces; taste knows when the rule is stale — propose a new token when a value accretes across >=3 components in a rolling 7-day window without being reverted.

nodes.label: Deterministic Detector / LLM Judgment Split
nodes.rationale: The deterministic layer owns detection, evidence, and confidence arithmetic; the LLM owns only classification, explanation, and bounded calibration — so the underline always fires on camera regardless of model latency.

nodes.label: Three-Way TASTE Verdict Taxonomy (regression / redesign / platform constraint)
nodes.norm_label: three-way taste verdict taxonomy (regression / redesign / platform constraint)

nodes.label: tokens.json (W3C Design Tokens source of truth)
nodes.norm_label: tokens.json (w3c design tokens source of truth)

nodes.label: 13px/12px Punchline Numeric Inconsistency
nodes.rationale: Verified defect: the demo script's seeded agent writes padding 13px while the promote verdict cites 12px — an internal incoherence on the flagship beat that resolved Implementation to 4.5 instead of 5.

nodes.label: Driftline (Design-System Reviewer That Never Gets Tired)
nodes.norm_label: driftline (design-system reviewer that never gets tired)

nodes.label: Bidirectional Reconciliation (tell the designer they're wrong)
nodes.rationale: When evidence shows code moved on purpose, draft a token-change proposal aimed at the designer instead of patching code — the concrete mechanism for 'keeping designers and engineers aligned'.
nodes.norm_label: bidirectional reconciliation (tell the designer they're wrong)

nodes.label: Verdict Flip on Evidence (live, on screen)
nodes.rationale: Layer 2 overturns Layer 1's naive distance-based read when commit corroboration and migration ratio point the other way — the visible distinction between classification and reasoning.
nodes.norm_label: verdict flip on evidence (live, on screen)

nodes.label: Recurrence-as-Vote (codebase voting for a missing token)
nodes.rationale: The same off-token value in three unrelated components isn't three bugs — it's the codebase voting for a token that doesn't exist yet; turns Promote-to-token from a button into a volunteered recommendation.
nodes.norm_label: recurrence-as-vote (codebase voting for a missing token)

nodes.label: Four-Verdict Taxonomy (regression / redesign / platform constraint / orphaned token)
nodes.norm_label: four-verdict taxonomy (regression / redesign / platform constraint / orphaned token)

nodes.label: Deterministic Core / Bounded LLM Architecture
nodes.rationale: Detection, diff, and the three corroborating signals are zero-LLM; the model touches only classification and fix-copy, constrained to scanner-verified byte ranges so a hallucinated patch is structurally impossible.

nodes.label: Corroborating Signals (migration ratio, commit corroboration, recurrence)
nodes.norm_label: corroborating signals (migration ratio, commit corroboration, recurrence)

nodes.label: Taste (Reviewer That Finishes the Fix — completion-centered)
nodes.norm_label: taste (reviewer that finishes the fix — completion-centered)

nodes.label: Completion / Migration Centerpiece (finish the half-done redesign)
nodes.rationale: Detection is table stakes (Percy/Chromatic solved it); the new category is 'finish my half-done redesign everywhere it hasn't landed yet, in one click, gated behind a human confirm' — a grid of stale components snapping into alignment ending on a real Cursor-opened PR.
nodes.norm_label: completion / migration centerpiece (finish the half-done redesign)

nodes.label: Three Boundary Signals (consistency ratio, co-commit clustering, token-distance)
nodes.norm_label: three boundary signals (consistency ratio, co-commit clustering, token-distance)

nodes.label: AMBIGUOUS / ASK Refusal Verdict
nodes.rationale: A reviewer with taste knows when NOT to decide: when the boundary signals disagree, the system surfaces competing hypotheses and defers to the human instead of forcing a guess — proven adversarially with a planted case engineered to fool a threshold-only tool.

nodes.label: .cursor/rules/design-system.mdc Dual-Use Constitution
nodes.rationale: One artifact is both the rubric the LLM judge scores against and the instruction set Cursor's agent follows when writing the fix — judge and fixer can never disagree about what 'correct' means.

nodes.label: Evidence Graph (tokens → CSS vars → components → interactive states)
nodes.norm_label: evidence graph (tokens → css vars → components → interactive states)

nodes.label: Cursor Background Agent (multi-file MIGRATE with PR-on-completion)
nodes.norm_label: cursor background agent (multi-file migrate with pr-on-completion)

nodes.label: Plan-Gated Conditional Pipeline
nodes.rationale: A structured Claude planning call per circuit decides which optional stages (exclusion review, billing cross-check) actually run, so clean and messy circuits visibly take different paths — 'Plan' as a load-bearing control-flow decision, not a label, without an open-ended agent loop.

nodes.label: Judgment-Triggered Exclusion-Clause Second Retrieval
nodes.rationale: A second retrieval fires only when the plan judged an interval ambiguous (e.g. 'scheduled maintenance' without pre-notification) — and visibly does not fire when it isn't, proving branching is data-driven, not scripted.

nodes.label: Deterministic Python Calculators (LLM never does arithmetic)
nodes.rationale: uptime_calculator, credit_calculator, billing_crosscheck, deadline_check, clause_precedence_resolver — every dollar and every rule-backed decision runs in Python; the model only reads and cites.
nodes.norm_label: deterministic python calculators (llm never does arithmetic)

nodes.label: Published Confidence Formula (0.4 retrieval + 0.4 ambiguity + 0.2 billing)
nodes.norm_label: published confidence formula (0.4 retrieval + 0.4 ambiguity + 0.2 billing)

nodes.label: Cross-Document Reconciliation Under Legal Effective Dates
nodes.rationale: Real facilities accumulate amendments that legally supersede parts of the original agreement; the agent must determine which vintage governs the tested quarter before computing — reconciling two real documents against a calendar, a harder version of the RAG-vs-agent line.
nodes.norm_label: cross-document reconciliation under legal effective dates

nodes.label: Itemized Confidence Deductions (100 minus disclosed penalties)
nodes.norm_label: itemized confidence deductions (100 minus disclosed penalties)

nodes.label: Vultr Managed Postgres + pgvector Precedent Store
nodes.norm_label: vultr managed postgres + pgvector precedent store

nodes.label: Real Backtest Against SEC Filings
nodes.rationale: Both documents and the near-breach are genuine EDGAR filings on a company that actually later amended its loan, so the agent's verdict is checkable against the public record — a backtest, not a scripted gotcha.

nodes.label: Discrepancy-Triggered Amendment Retrieval
nodes.rationale: The recomputed ratio disagreeing with the borrower's self-reported figure beyond a 0.02x tolerance is the trigger — not a script — for a second retrieval into amendment history; the discrepancy appears on screen before the retrieval it causes, the legible RAG-vs-agent difference.

nodes.label: Deterministic Precedence Resolution (filing-date comparison, not LLM)
nodes.norm_label: deterministic precedence resolution (filing-date comparison, not llm)

nodes.label: Backwards calc-match Confidence Semantics
nodes.rationale: The calc-match sub-score rewards proximity to the borrower's self-reported number, but the product's thesis is that the counterparty can be wrong — a correct agent that legitimately diverges from a wrong borrower would be penalized exactly when it should be most confident.

nodes.label: Air Boss (Flight-Director Copilot for Drone Light Shows)
nodes.norm_label: air boss (flight-director copilot for drone light shows)

nodes.label: Cluster-vs-Isolated Cause Classification
nodes.rationale: A deviation shared by a cluster of neighboring drones is a formation-wide event (wind); one drone diverging while neighbors stay nominal is an isolated motor/GPS fault — the two demand opposite actions (group HOLD/REROUTE vs single-drone DESCEND-RTL).

nodes.label: Deterministic Preference-Table Learning Loop
nodes.rationale: Overrides are written to a timestamped log that a local ranker reads before Crusoe is ever called, so the learned preference swap is guaranteed to fire on cue — not a stochastic bet on few-shot prompting; Crusoe only narrates the swap.

nodes.label: Crusoe Managed Inference (Gemma-3-12B primary, Llama-3.3-70B fallback)
nodes.norm_label: crusoe managed inference (gemma-3-12b primary, llama-3.3-70b fallback)

nodes.label: LLM-as-Narrator Safety Pattern (never touches a number)
nodes.rationale: Physics, severity, cause, and action ranking are deterministic code; Crusoe only turns the result into a sentence with why-chips citing provided facts verbatim — pre-answering 'isn't the AI making up the safety numbers?'.
nodes.norm_label: llm-as-narrator safety pattern (never touches a number)

nodes.label: FAA Part 107.35 Waiver (human abort call in federal law)
nodes.norm_label: faa part 107.35 waiver (human abort call in federal law)

nodes.label: Pump-vs-Load Causal Disambiguation
nodes.rationale: Coolant delta-T climbing while GPU temp is still nominal reads as pump degradation, not load — a signal-shape inference a single-metric threshold alarm structurally cannot make.

nodes.label: Constraint-Aware Action Set (sometimes do nothing)
nodes.rationale: A non-checkpointed, high-SLA job can't be safely moved, so the right move is a power-cap or hold — reframing the naive 'always migrate' reflex.

nodes.label: Cross-Rack Contagion Foresight (shared cooling-loop headroom)
nodes.rationale: Racks sharing a loop share a cooling_capacity pool, so every candidate fix is scored on the headroom it frees for neighbors — making contagion a computed claim, not a narrative flourish.

nodes.label: Computed Confidence (margin + headroom + precedent, never LLM-authored)
nodes.norm_label: computed confidence (margin + headroom + precedent, never llm-authored)

nodes.label: Translation-Latency Reframe (failure mode is not missing data)
nodes.rationale: On a fire the failure mode is translation latency — too many feeds and not enough trusted judgment delivered fast enough to act on; the deadliest event is a wind shift invalidating an escape route faster than radio chatter can track it (Yarnell Hill, 2013).
nodes.norm_label: translation-latency reframe (failure mode is not missing data)

nodes.label: Hazard-Agnostic Safety-Margin Bias (override learning that generalizes)
nodes.rationale: An override widens a per-crew safety margin that later triggers an earlier warning on an unrelated hazard type (spot fire, not front advance) — adaptation of a policy, not memorization of one fact; the one part a pattern-cloner doesn't get by copying the brief.
nodes.norm_label: hazard-agnostic safety-margin bias (override learning that generalizes)

nodes.label: Single Situation Card (one map, one sentence, one tap)
nodes.norm_label: single situation card (one map, one sentence, one tap)

nodes.label: Two Crusoe Call Patterns (single-shot critical path vs function-calling Why path)
nodes.rationale: The timing-critical countdown path uses one JSON-mode request with pre-fetched state (zero tool round-trips gambled against the clock); genuine multi-step function-calling is reserved for the operator-initiated Why path where 2-3s is affordable.
nodes.norm_label: two crusoe call patterns (single-shot critical path vs function-calling why path)

nodes.label: Three-Primitive Causal Chain (Live Translate → Antigravity → Computer Use)
nodes.rationale: Each primitive fires only because the previous one is running: Computer Use has no trigger without Antigravity's red-flag threshold; the spoken confirmation has nothing to say without Computer Use's success — the exact shape the track rewards.
nodes.norm_label: three-primitive causal chain (live translate → antigravity → computer use)

nodes.label: Cold-Restart Persistence Proof (the kill on camera)
nodes.rationale: Destroying the process on stage and resuming from Antigravity persistent state is the unfakeable rebuttal to 'forgets the moment the task ends' — a 'Day 2' title card can be staged; a killed process cannot.
nodes.norm_label: cold-restart persistence proof (the kill on camera)

nodes.label: CHF Red-Flag Protocol (weight gain, orthopnea thresholds)
nodes.norm_label: chf red-flag protocol (weight gain, orthopnea thresholds)

nodes.label: ClaimThread (FNOL Claims Agent with Persistent Claim File)
nodes.norm_label: claimthread (fnol claims agent with persistent claim file)

nodes.label: Teardown-and-Resume Proof (different rep, different machine)
nodes.rationale: The process is killed on camera and a different rep on a different machine resumes the exact claim by environment ID — strictly harder than the brief's scheduled-resume example because the lookup key is a claimant/policy match spoken live that must resolve to the right env_id.

nodes.label: Workforce Fungibility (any rep picks up mid-file)
nodes.rationale: The provable claim isn't 'the same bot remembers' — it's that any employee can pick up mid-file, the actual staffing problem a CAT-surge carrier has (throw fresh headcount at a spike, not clone one bot).
nodes.norm_label: workforce fungibility (any rep picks up mid-file)

nodes.label: Deterministic Completeness Rule (CHECK is code, not LLM)
nodes.rationale: Whether the claim file is enough to act on is decided by a rule over required fields and the document checklist, never an LLM guess — the only non-deterministic steps are translation quality and click-grounding, both behind a human-confirm gate.
nodes.norm_label: deterministic completeness rule (check is code, not llm)

nodes.label: Multi-Step CRUD Claim Lifecycle (open, update, mark ready)
nodes.norm_label: multi-step crud claim lifecycle (open, update, mark ready)

nodes.label: Cross-Language Callback Hero Beat
nodes.rationale: Two days later a different caregiver calls about the same patient in a different language, and the agent surfaces Monday's open flag unprompted mid-sentence — a moment a snapshot chatbot physically cannot produce; the persistence thesis staged as one beat.

nodes.label: Evidence Clause on Every Fact Card
nodes.rationale: Every extracted card shows the source-language clause beside the English fact, so the one-tap confirm is a human sanity-check on the translation as well as the action — the mistranslation failure mode caught by design.
nodes.norm_label: evidence clause on every fact card

nodes.label: Deliberately Legacy-Styled Care Portal (Computer Use target)
nodes.norm_label: deliberately legacy-styled care portal (computer use target)

nodes.label: Reclaim API Endpoints (health, cases, corpus, run-portfolio, run, events SSE, memo, approve, approvals)
nodes.norm_label: reclaim api endpoints (health, cases, corpus, run-portfolio, run, events sse, memo, approve, approvals)

nodes.norm_label: reclaim frontend redesign implications (linear/retool hybrid direction)

nodes.norm_label: claim window clause highlight (msa section 9.1, 30-day claim window, citation id claim-window)

nodes.label: Invoice Credit Line Highlight (BAN 77104, prior SLA credit -$1,840 for CKT-DEN-031, citation id invoice-credit)
nodes.norm_label: invoice credit line highlight (ban 77104, prior sla credit -$1,840 for ckt-den-031, citation id invoice-credit)
